title: To amend the Federal Water Pollution Control Act with respect to permitting terms, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules for water pollution permits under federal law. It allows states and cities to get permits that last for up to ten years instead of shorter time periods. Other organizations like businesses can get permits that last for up to five years. This means permit holders won't have to renew their permits as often as they did before.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Water Resources and Environment.

sections.title: Section 1 National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) Terms
sections.text: This section changes how long water pollution permits last. The law already has a system called the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System or NPDES. This system gives permits to people and organizations that want to discharge pollutants into water. The new rules say that states and cities can get permits that last up to ten years. All other people and organizations can get permits that last up to five years. The section also fixes some small writing errors in the existing law. These fixes change references to make them more consistent and correct capitalization in a few places.
